name: Manu Bhaker
medals: 🥉 Paris 2024 — 10m Air Pistol; 🥉 Paris 2024 — Mixed Team; 🥇 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 CWG 2022; 🥇 World Championships 2018
records: First Indian woman to win an Olympic shooting medal. First athlete from independent India to win two medals at a single Olympics. Wikipedia Domestic ranking avg: 578 (10m air pistol) per NRAI 2025 trials. Most decorated Indian woman shooter ever.

name: Swapnil Kusale
medals: 🥉 Paris 2024 — 50m Rifle 3P (India's first-ever medal in this event); 🥇 Asian Games 2023
records: India's first-ever Olympic medal in 50m rifle 3 positions. SPORT Career-best score: 594(qualification round, Asian Games 2023). Competed in 3-position event requiring kneeling, prone, and standing — all in sequence.

name: Sarabjot Singh
medals: 🥉 Paris 2024 — Mixed Team (with Manu Bhaker); 🥇 ISSF World Cup 2023 Baku
records: Career-best qualification score: 583 (10m air pistol). First Indian mixed team shooting Olympic medallist. World Cup gold at age 21.

name: Arjun Babuta
medals: 4th place Paris 2024 (missed bronze by 0.1 points); 🥇 ISSF World Cup 2024
records: World-ranked shooter. Athletiqconnect Career-best qualification score: 632.5 (10m air rifle). 4th at Olympics by 0.1 point — most heartbreaking Indian shooting result at Paris 2024. World No. 1 in 10m air rifle in 2024.

name: Elavenil Valarivan
medals: 🥇 ISSF World Cup 2020 Munich; 🥇 ISSF World Cup 2021; Paris 2024 finalist
records: Ranked No. 2 nationally in 10m air rifle women with domestic average of 633.28. Wikipedia Career-best score: 253.4 (final score — world-class level). Former world No. 1 at age 20.

name: Sift Kaur Samra
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2023; Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 National Games 2025 (50m 3P)
records: Won Asian Games gold with a new world record score of 469.6 in the final, also setting Asian Games record of 594 in qualification. SportsphilanthropynetworkRemains the top-ranked 3P women's shooter in India with national average of 593.38. Wikipedia Current world record holder 50m rifle 3P.

name: Ramita Jindal
medals: Paris 2024 finalist (top 8); 🥇 ISSF World Cup 2023
records: Ranked 5th nationally in 10m air rifle women with domestic average 632.05. Wikipedia Career-best final score: 228.1. World Cup gold at 19. Youngest shooter in Paris 2024 India squad.

name: Anjum Moudgil
medals: 🥈 World Championships 2018; Paris 2024 participant
records: Back in national reckoning for 50m 3P with average of 591.68 — 3rd nationally. Wikipedia 2-time Olympian. Career-best score: 593 (qualification). India's most experienced women's rifle shooter.

name: Rhythm Sangwan
medals: 🥇 ISSF Junior World Championships 2021; Paris 2024 participant
records: Career-best score: 580 (10m air pistol qualification). Junior World Champion. Strong senior transition — Paris Olympian at 21.

name: Anish Bhanwala
medals: 🥇 CWG 2018 (youngest CWG shooting gold medalist — age 15); Paris 2024 participant
records: Record: Youngest-ever CWG shooting gold medalist at age 15 (2018). Career-best score in RFP finals. 10-year window remaining until peak career.

name: Vijayveer Sidhu
medals: 🥇 ISSF World Cup 2022 Cairo; Paris 2024 participant
records: Career-best score: 30 hits (rapid fire pistol final — world-class level). World Cup gold at 21. Consistent top-8 internationally.

name: Esha Singh
medals: 🥇 ISSF Junior World Championships 2023; 🥇 Asian Games 2023 (mixed team)
records: Career-best score: 579 (10m air pistol). Junior World Champion at 18. Selected for Paris 2024 squad in women's 25m pistol alongside Manu Bhaker. Athletiqconnect India's next air pistol star.

name: Aishwary Pratap Singh Tomar
medals: 🥇 ISSF World Cup 2021 Osijek; Paris 2024 participant
records: Career-best qualification score: 590 (50m rifle 3P). Selected for Paris 2024 squad in 50m rifle 3P. Athletiqconnect Multiple World Cup medals.

name: Arjun Cheema
medals: 🥇 ISSF World Cup 2023 Rio; Paris 2024 participant
records: Career-best score: 581 (10m air pistol qualification). World Cup gold medalist. Consistent top-8 finisher internationally.

name: Prithviraj Tondaiman
medals: 🥇 CWG 2022 (mixed team); Paris 2024 participant
records: Career-best score: 74/75 (trap qualification — world-class level). First Indian trap shooter at Olympics in years.

name: Maheshwari Chauhan
medals: 🥇 CWG 2022 (mixed team); Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 National Games 2025 (skeet women)
records: Career-best score: 53 (skeet final — won gold at 38th National Games with score of 53 in women's skeet final.) Theathletedp Veteran skeet shooter.

name: Tilottama Sen
medals: 🥇 ISSF Junior World Championships 2024
records: Career-best score: 634 (10m air rifle qualification — junior world-class). Junior World Champion at 16. India's youngest active world champion in shooting.

name: Simranpreet Kaur Brar
medals: 🥇 ISSF World Cup Final 2025 (junior women's 25m pistol)
records: Won gold in junior women's 25m sports pistol at 68th National Shooting Championship with score of 39 in the final. workinp World Cup Final gold at 18. Emerging 25m pistol pipeline.

name: Anantjeet Singh Naruka
medals: 🥈 Asian Games 2023; Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 National Games (skeet)
records: Career-best score: 56 in skeet final — won gold at National Games with score of 56. TheathletedpAsian Games silver. India's top active skeet shooter.

name: Mehuli Ghosh
medals: 🥇 Asian Championships 2024 (10m air rifle); 🥈 CWG 2018; Back to national No. 1
records: Asian Championship 2024 gold medalist — returned to No. 1 spot in domestic rankings with average of 633.65. Wikipedia Career-best score: 252.9 (final). India's current top-ranked 10m air rifle woman.

name: Neeraj Chopra
medals: 🥇 Tokyo 2020; 🥈 Paris 2024; 🥇 World Championships 2023; 🥇 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 Diamond League Final 2022
records: Rewrote his own national record with 90.23m at the 2025 Doha Diamond League — first Indian to breach the 90m mark. Crunchbase Previous NR: 89.94m (Stockholm DL 2022). World rank #2. India's greatest track and field athlete.

name: Avinash Sable
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2023; 🥈 Asian Championships 2023; Paris 2024 finalist
records: National record: 8:09.91 set at Paris Diamond League 2024. Two Circles Also holds half marathon NR: sub-61 minutes (first Indian under 61 mins). 6x national record breaker. India's greatest-ever distance runner.

name: Jyothi Yarraji
medals: 🥇 Asian Indoor Championships 2024; 🥈 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 Asian Athletics Championships 2023
records: National record: 12.78 seconds — first set at FISU World University Games 2023 in Chengdu, matched at 2024 Motonet GP Jyvaskyla in Finland. Crunchbase Second-fastest Asian hurdler. Defending Asian champion.

name: Tajinderpal Singh Toor
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 Asian Championships 2023; 🥇 Asian Indoor 2024
records: National record: 21.77m — achieved at Inter-State Athletics Championships in Bhubaneswar on June 19, 2023. Two Circles Asian record. Only Indian to throw 21m+. Three-time Asian champion.

name: Murali Sreeshankar
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥈 Asian Games 2023
records: National record: 8.36m (set 2022). Career-best: consistent 8.20m+ jumps. India's best-ever long jumper. Consistent top-6 globally.

name: Parul Chaudhary
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2023 (5000m); 🥈 Asian Games 2023 (steeplechase); Paris 2024 (both events)
records: National record in 3000m steeplechase: 9:12.46— achieved at Asian Athletics Championships 2025. Crunchbase Also holds the 5000m NR. Only Indian to qualify for two individual track events at Paris 2024. Crunchbase

name: Annu Rani
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 Asian Athletics 2023; Paris 2024 participant
records: National record: 63.82m — set at Indian Open Javelin Throw Competition 2022. CrunchbaseFirst Indian woman to throw 60m+. Asian champion.

name: Jeswin Aldrin
medals: Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 World Athletics Relays 2024
records: National record holder in long jump. LinkedInPersonal best: 8.42m — national record, bettering Sreeshankar's mark. U23 star. India's highest long jump leap ever.

name: Praveen Chithravel
medals: 🥉 Asian Athletics Championships 2025; World Athletics regular
records: Matched his own national record with 17.37mleap at 2025 Asian Athletics Championships. LinkedIn India's best-ever triple jumper.

name: Abdulla Aboobacker
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; Paris 2024 participant; Reigning Asian triple jump champion
records: Personal best: 17.19m — former national record. Reigning Asian champion in triple jump. LinkedIn India's best-ever international triple jump result.

name: Harmilan Bains
medals: 🥇 Asian Indoor 2024 (1500m); 🥈 Asian Games 2023 (both events)
records: National record 1500m: 4:05.39. National record 800m: 2:00.19. Daughter of Olympian Madhuri Singh. India's best middle-distance woman.

name: Priyanka Goswami
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Race Walking 2024; Paris 2024
records: National record: 1:28:45 — set at National Open Race Walking Championships, February 14, 2021. Two Circles India's best-ever women's race walker.

name: Vithya Ramraj
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2023 (relay); Paris 2024 relay
records: Matched PT Usha's legendary 400m hurdles national record of 55.42 seconds at Asian Games 2023 in Hangzhou heats Crunchbase — a record that had stood 39 years.

name: Tejaswin Shankar
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022 (high jump); Paris 2024 (decathlon)
records: Decathlon national record: 8,057 points — achieved at 2026 Federation Cup in Ranchi. Crunchbase Also holds high jump NR: 2.29m. India's only world-class decathlete.

name: Gurindervir Singh
medals: 🥇 Federation Cup 2026 (100m)
records: Reclaimed the 100m national record with 10.09 seconds at Federation Cup 2026 in Ranchi. Crunchbase India's fastest man. First Indian to break 10.10 seconds officially.

name: Kishore Jena
medals: Paris 2024 participant; 🥈 Asian Games 2023
records: Personal best: 87.54m — closest Indian to Neeraj Chopra. India's #2 javelin thrower. Strong 2028 contender.

name: Shaili Singh
medals: 🥈 World U20 Championships 2022; 🥇 Asian U20 2023
records: Personal best: 6.76m — national U20 record. Trained by PT Usha at TOPS academy. India's top U23 women's long jumper globally.

name: Suraj Panwar
medals: 🥇 World U20 Race Walk 2022; Paris 2024 participant
records: Personal best: 1:19:47 (20km race walk) — set at Paris 2024. World U20 Race Walk champion at 17. India's youngest Paris 2024 athletics representative.

name: Yashvir Singh
medals: 🥇 Indian Open Athletics 2025; Asian Athletics Championships 2025 representative
records: Personal best: 82.57m — set at Gumi Asian Athletics Championships 2025. Two CirclesTop-5 Indian javelin thrower. 2028 Olympic pipeline.

name: Rohan Yadav
medals: 🥇 Asian U18 Athletics 2024
records: Personal best: 76.40m (U18 national record). U17/U18 javelin star. Coaches identify as the most likely next Neeraj. Enormous 2032 potential.

name: Aman Sehrawat
medals: 🥉 Paris 2024 Olympics; 🥇 Asian Championships 2023; 🥉 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 U23 World Championships 2022
records: India's youngest-ever individual Olympic medallist. Highest world ranking: No. 2 (August 19, 2024). Two Circles Career record: Olympic bronze + U23 World gold + Asian champion — all by age 21. Trained at Chhatrasal alongside Sushil Kumar, Ravi Dahiya, Bajrang Punia.

name: Antim Panghal
medals: 🥉 World Championships 2023; 🥇 U20 World Championships 2022 + 2023; 🥉 Asian Games 2023
records: Highest world ranking: No. 1 (2025). World Championship finalist 2023 and 2025. BloombergIndia's first-ever U20 world champion — also first to defend the title. WSC Sports Career record: Back-to-back U20 World golds + Senior World bronze + World No. 1.

name: Bajrang Punia
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥇 CWG 2018 + 2022; 🥇 Asian Games 2018; 🥉 World Championships 2022
records: 4 World Championship medals — only Indian wrestler with this record. PLOS Career record: Olympic bronze + 4 World medals + 2 CWG golds + 2 Asian Games golds. Most decorated active Indian male wrestler historically. Currently in political controversy.

name: Ravi Kumar Dahiya
medals: 🥈 Tokyo 2020; 🥇 Asian Championships 2020 + 2021 + 2022 (three consecutive)
records: Career record: Olympic silver + 3 consecutive Asian titles + World Championships bronze 2019. 3 consecutive Asian Championships golds is an Indian record in wrestling. Injury kept him out of Paris 2024.

name: Vinesh Phogat
medals: Paris 2024 — DQ from gold final; 🥇 CWG 2018 + 2022; 🥇 Asian Games 2018 + 2022; 🥈 World Championships 2018
records: Career record: Most decorated Indian woman wrestler— 2 Asian Games golds, 2 CWG golds, World silver, World bronze. DQ from gold at Paris 2024 by 100 grams overweight. Retired post-Paris.

name: Anshu Malik
medals: 🥈 World Championships 2021; 🥇 CWG 2022; Paris 2024 participant
records: Record: First Indian woman to win World Championships silver (2021 at age 19). Career-best world ranking: No. 3 (57kg). CWG champion.

name: Deepak Punia
medals: 🥈 World Championships 2019; 🥇 CWG 2022; Paris 2024 participant
records: Record: World Championships silver at 19 — youngest-ever Indian male World Championship finalist. CWG champion. Career-best world ranking: Top 5 in 86kg.

name: Mansi Ahlawat
medals: 🥉 World Championships 2024 Tirana; 🥇 Asian U23 2024
records: Career record: Senior World bronze at 21 + Asian U23 gold in same year. India's most recent World Championship medallist in wrestling. Career-best world ranking: Top 8 (59kg).

name: Reetika Hooda
medals: Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Championships 2022
records: Career record: CWG gold + Asian championship gold in same year at 21. Career-best world ranking: Top 6 (76kg). One of India's strongest women's wrestlers physically.

name: Nisha Dahiya
medals: Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 Asian Championships 2021
records: Career record: Asian champion at 21. Career-best world ranking: Top 10 (68kg). Strong senior pipeline.

name: Priya Malik
medals: 🥇 U20 World Championships 2021
records: Record: Junior World Champion at 17. Career-best ranking: Top 15 U20 globally. Enormous long-term potential — will peak at 2028–2032.

name: Jyoti Berwal
medals: 🥇 U20 World Championships 2024; 🥇 Asian U20 2024
records: Record: Double U20 gold (World + Asian) in 2024 — only Indian wrestler to do both in same year at U20. India's brightest current U20 wrestling talent.

name: Sushil Kumar
medals: 🥇 World Championships 2010; 🥉 Athens 2004; 🥈 London 2012
records: Record: India's only Wrestling World Champion. Only Indian to win 2 individual Olympic medals before Neeraj Chopra. Career: Olympic bronze + Olympic silver + World gold. Legal issues have sidelined him.

name: Mohit Grewal
medals: 🥇 CWG 2022; 🥈 Asian Championships 2023
records: Career record: CWG champion + Asian silver. Career-best world ranking: Top 8 (125kg). India's best-ever super heavyweight wrestler.

name: Gourav Baliyan
medals: 🥇 Asian Championships 2020; 🥈 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: Asian champion + Asian Games silver. Career-best world ranking: Top 8 (79kg). Consistent international performer.

name: Pooja Gehlot
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Championships 2022
records: Career record: Asian champion + CWG silver. Career-best world ranking: Top 10 (53kg). Strong 2028 candidate.

name: Nitika Dahiya
medals: 🥇 U20 World Championships 2024
records: Record: Junior World Champion 2024 at age 17. Career-best: World No. 1 U20 (62kg). Enormous long-term potential — 2032 Olympic target.

name: Sachin Malik
medals: 🥇 U20 World Championships 2024
records: Record: India's best-ever Greco-Roman result at U20 World level. Junior world champion 2024. First significant Indian success in Greco-Roman in years.

name: Sujeet Kalkal
medals: 🥈 U23 World Championships 2022; 🥇 Asian U23 2022
records: Career record: U23 World silver + Asian U23 gold. Career-best world ranking: Top 12 (74kg, U23). Strong transition to senior level underway.

name: Divya Kakran
medals: 🥇 Asian Championships 2021; 🥈 CWG 2022
records: Career record: Asian champion + CWG silver. Career-best world ranking: Top 10 (68kg). Veteran of Indian women's wrestling.

name: PV Sindhu
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥇 World Championships 2019; 🥈 World Championships 2018; 🥈 Rio 2016; 🥇 CWG 2018 + 2022
records: Career-best world ranking: World No. 2 (2017). 5 World Championship medals — most by any Indian badminton player. 2-time Olympic medallist. Most decorated Indian woman athlete.

name: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy
medals: Paris 2024 QF; 🥇 Asian Games 2023; 🥉 World Championships 2023; 🥇 Indonesia Open 2023 (Super 1000)
records: Holds the record for longest duration as World No. 1 among Indian badminton players — surpassed Saina Nehwal's record of nine consecutive weeks at the top in May 2024. The Week Career-best: World No. 1doubles (with Chirag). First Indian doubles pair to reach world No. 1. Wikipedia

name: Chirag Shetty
medals: Paris 2024 QF; 🥇 Asian Games 2023; World No. 1 doubles (with Satwik)
records: Together with Satwik, holds the record for longest duration as World No. 1 in Indian badminton history. The Week Career-best: World No. 1 (2023–24). Reigning Asian Games champions. Wikipedia First Indian pair to win Super 1000 (Indonesia Open 2023).

name: Lakshya Sen
medals: 4th place Paris 2024 (semifinal); 🥉 World Championships 2021; 🥇 CWG 2022
records: Career-best world ranking: World No. 6 (2022). Broke into world top 10 with career-best of No. 6. pressreader First Indian men's singles shuttler to reach Olympic semifinal (Paris 2024). CWG champion at 20.

name: HS Prannoy
medals: 🥉 World Championships 2023; Paris 2024 R16; 🥇 French Open 2022
records: Career-best world ranking: World No. 6 (2018 + matched 2023). World Championships bronze at 31 — career peak performance. Global Times Most consistent Indian men's singles player of the 2022–24 era.

name: Kidambi Srikanth
medals: 🥈 World Championships 2021; 4x Super Series titles; Former world No. 1
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 1 (January 2018) — one of only three Indians to have held world No. 1 in badminton (with Prakash Padukone 1980 and Saina Nehwal 2015). The Week First Indian man to reach World Championships final.

name: Saina Nehwal
medals: 🥉 London 2012; 🥇 CWG 2010 + 2018; Former world No. 1
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 1 (2015). First Indian to achieve World No. 1 in women's singles. The WeekIndia's first Olympic badminton medallist. 5 World Championship medals. Pioneer of Indian women's badminton.

name: Treesa Jolly
medals: 🥇 India Open 2022; Multiple Super 500 wins; 🥈 Uber Cup 2022
records: Career-best world ranking: World No. 11 (2024) — achieved career-best world ranking of 11 in BWF World Rankings with partner Gayatri Gopichand. tribuneindia Youngest Indian to win a BWF Super 500 title.

name: Gayatri Gopichand
medals: 🥇 India Open 2022; Multiple Super 500 wins; 🥈 Uber Cup 2022
records: Career-best: World No. 11 (with Treesa). Daughter of coach Pullela Gopichand. First daughter of a top coach to represent India at this level.

name: Ashwini Ponnappa
medals: 🥇 CWG 2010 (mixed doubles); 🥇 Asian Championships 2024 (doubles); Paris 2024
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 8 (mixed doubles with Jwala Gutta, 2011). India's most experienced active doubles player — 15+ year international career.

name: Tanisha Crasto
medals: 🥈 Asian Championships 2023 (mixed doubles); Paris 2024 participant
records: Career-best world ranking: World No. 18 (mixed doubles with Ishaan Bhatnagar). Youngest player in India's Paris 2024 squad. High future potential.

name: Unnati Hooda
medals: 🥇 Dutch Open 2023 (Super 100); Ranked inside world top 100 at age 16
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 67 at age 16. Youngest Indian player to win an international title. tribuneindiaIndia's most exciting U17 star.

name: Ayush Shetty
medals: 🥇 World Junior Championships 2023
records: Record: World Junior Champion at 17 (2023) — first Indian men's singles junior world champion. Career-best junior ranking: World No. 1 junior. Most exciting U18 singles prospect India has produced in years.

name: MR Arjun
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2023 (team); Multiple Super 500 results
records: Career-best doubles ranking: World No. 25 (with Dhruv Kapila). Asian Games team gold member.

name: Malvika Bansod
medals: Multiple Super 100 titles; National top-3
records: Career-best world ranking: World No. 46. Consistent top-50 performer. Strong women's singles successor to Sindhu.

name: Priyanshu Rajawat
medals: 🥇 Orleans Masters 2023; Multiple Super 100 titles
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 28. India's 3rd-ranked men's singles — strong 2028 LA contender.

name: Kiran George
medals: Multiple Super 100 titles; National top-5
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 52. Consistent Super 100/300 winner. Possible 2028 pipeline.

name: Tasnim Mir
medals: 🥇 Bulgaria International 2022; Former world No. 1 junior
records: Record: World No. 1 junior ranking at age 16 — highest-ever ranking by an Indian U17 women's singles player. Currently transitioning to senior level.

name: Ishaan Bhatnagar
medals: 🥈 Asian Championships 2023 (mixed doubles with Tanisha); Paris 2024 squad
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 18 (mixed doubles). India's top mixed doubles male player.

name: Aditi Bhatt
medals: 🥇 Asian U17 Championships 2024
records: Career-best: Asian U17 champion 2024. Currently under Gopichand's direct coaching. India's top U17 women's singles player. 2032 Olympic target.

name: Harmanpreet Singh
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 Asian Champions Trophy 2024
records: Scored 10 goals in 8 matches at Paris 2024 — tournament's top scorer. ESPN Scored more than 200 international goals for India. sportskeeda 3-time FIH Player of the Year (2021, 2022, 2024) — no other Indian has won it more than twice. ESPN India's highest-scoring modern-era player.

name: PR Sreejesh
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 CWG 2014
records: Won Goalkeeper of the Year at FIH Hockey Stars Awards 2024 for the third time — 2021, 2022, and 2024. ESPNRecord: 3-time FIH Goalkeeper of the Year — most by any Asian goalkeeper. 3-time Olympian. Retired after Paris 2024 on a bronze medal. Greatest Indian goalkeeper ever.

name: Manpreet Singh
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023; FIH Player of Year 2019
records: Career record: FIH Player of the Year 2019 — only Indian to win it in that decade. 3-time Olympian. 200+ international caps. India's greatest-ever midfielder.

name: Vivek Sagar Prasad
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: 2 Olympic bronze medals by age 25 — only Indian hockey player to achieve this at that age. FIH Rising Star nominee 2021. India's most dynamic young midfielder.

name: Amit Rohidas
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: 3-time Olympian + 2 Olympic bronze medals. Known for the highest drag-flick speed measured in Indian hockey at national camps. Core of India's back four.

name: Mandeep Singh
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: 2 Olympic bronze medals + Asian Games gold. 100+ international goals for India. Key forward across both Olympic campaigns.

name: Nilakanta Sharma
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: 2 Olympic bronze medals. 150+ international caps. Consistent box-to-box midfielder across both Olympic cycles.

name: Abhishek
medals: Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 Asian Games 2023; FIH Rising Star 2023
records: One of the new players who shone brightly — fourth-highest goal-scorer in the FIH Pro League 2023. Olympics FIH Rising Star of the Year 2023 — highest honour for a young player globally in hockey. Youngest key player in Paris 2024 squad.

name: Jugraj Singh
medals: 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: Drag-flick conversion rate of 73% in penalty corners at Paris 2024 — second-highest in the tournament. Harmanpreet's direct successor.

name: Sukhjit Singh
medals: 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: Emerged as first-choice left back at Paris 2024. 0 goals conceded in last 3 Paris 2024 matches when fully fit. Key to India's defensive solidity.

name: Salima Tete
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Games 2022; 🥇 Asian Champions Trophy 2023
records: Career record: Arjuna Award 2024 — youngest-ever hockey player to receive it at national level. 100+ international caps. India's best young women's player and captain.

name: Vandana Katariya
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Games 2022; Tokyo 2020
records: Record: India's all-time leading women's hockey goal scorer — 60+ international goals. First Indian woman to score a hat-trick at Olympics (Tokyo 2020 vs South Africa).

name: Gurjit Kaur
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Games 2022; Tokyo 2020
records: Record: Best drag-flick conversion rate in women's Asian hockey — penalty corner specialist with 70%+ conversion in major tournaments. India's premier women's drag-flicker.

name: Deep Grace Ekka
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Games 2022; 2-time Olympian
records: Career record: 2 Olympics + Asian Games gold + CWG silver. 150+ international caps. India's most experienced women's defender.

name: Navneet Kaur
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Games 2022
records: Career record: Top-3 scorer for India women's team in last 3 years. Explosive forward. Key to women's team attack across all major tournaments.

name: Lalit Upadhyay
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: 2 Olympic bronze medals + Asian Games gold. 100+ international goals. Clinical finisher across both Olympic campaigns.

name: Araijeet Singh Hundal
medals: Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 Junior Asia Cup 2023; 🥇 Junior World Cup 2023
records: Career record: Junior Asia Cup + Junior World Cup gold — best U21 forward record in Indian hockey in years. Already tested in Olympic environment at 19.

name: Sushila Chanu Pukhrambam
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥇 Asian Games 2022; 3-time Olympian
records: Record: 3 Olympic appearances — most by any active Indian women's hockey player. 200+ international caps. Most experienced active women's player.

name: Mumtaz Khan
medals: 🥇 Junior Asia Cup Women 2023; Rising women's team regular
records: Career record: Junior Asia Cup gold. India's most dynamic young women's forward. Will be at peak for 2028 and 2032 Olympics.

name: Hardik Singh
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥉 Paris 2024; 🥇 FIH Player of the Year 2023
records: Record: FIH Player of the Year 2023 ESPN — only Indian to win the award in back-to-back years (Indians won it 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024 consecutively). 2 Olympic bronze medals. India's most creative midfielder.

name: Lovlina Borgohain
medals: 🥉 Tokyo 2020; 🥇 World Championships 2023 (75kg); 🥉 Asian Games 2023; 🥇 CWG 2022
records: Only active Indian Olympic boxing medallist. One of only three Indian boxers to have won an Olympic medal — Vijender Singh bronze 2008, Mary Kom bronze 2012, Lovlina bronze Tokyo 2020. sportskeeda 2-time World Championships medallist (gold 2023, bronze 2019).

name: Nikhat Zareen
medals: 🥇 World Championships 2022 + 2023 (back-to-back); 🥇 CWG 2022; 🥉 Asian Games 2023; Paris 2024 R16
records: Just two bouts lost since 2022. Has beaten everyone in 52kg during that time. ESPN Only Indian boxer with back-to-back World Championship golds. Record: 2 World titles in consecutive years (2022, 2023) — no Indian boxer male or female has done this.

name: Nishant Dev
medals: Paris 2024 QF; 🥉 World Championships 2023; 🥇 Asian Championships 2024
records: First Indian boxer to secure Paris 2024 quota after making the semi-finals of the 71kg category at the World Olympic Boxing Qualifiers. ESPN Personal record: World Championships bronze at age 21. India's highest-ranked active men's boxer (71kg).

name: Amit Panghal
medals: 🥇 CWG 2022; 🥈 World Championships 2019; 🥇 Asian Games 2018; Paris 2024 R16
records: First Indian male boxer to win a World Championships silver medal. sportskeeda Former world No. 1 ranking in 52kg — highest ranking ever by an Indian male boxer.

name: Preeti Pawar
medals: Paris 2024 debut; 🥉 Asian Championships 2023; 🥉 Asian Games 2022
records: 3 international medals by age 22. Paris 2024 Olympic debutant. Best finish: Asian Championships bronze 2023.

name: Jaismine Lamboria
medals: 🥇 World Championships 2025 (57kg gold — beat Olympic silver medallist); 🥉 CWG 2022; Paris 2024 R32
records: Won 2025 World Championships gold in 57kg with a 4-1 split decision win over Poland's Julia Szeremeta, 2024 Olympic silver medallist. Olympics Career-defining win over an Olympic silver medallist.

name: Shiva Thapa
medals: 🥉 Asian Games 2014 + 2018; 5x Asian Championships medals; 🥉 CWG 2014
records: Five-time Asian medallist Olympics — most Asian medals by any Indian male boxer ever. Career record: 5 Asian medals across 3 different weight classes.

name: Deepak Bhoria
medals: 🥉 World Championships 2023; 🥈 Asian Championships 2023; 🥇 Asian Games 2023 (team)
records: World Championships bronze at 22. Career record: 3 major international medals in 12 months (2023). Fastest hands measured in Indian boxing at national camps.

name: Narender Berwal
medals: 🥈 Asian Championships 2023; 🥇 Youth World Championships 2021
records: Youth World Champion at 19. India's first Youth World champion in super heavyweight. Career record: 2 international medals — best super heavyweight record in Indian history.

name: Mary Kom
medals: 🥉 London 2012; 🥇 World Championships 2002, 2005, 2006, 2008, 2010, 2018 (6 golds); 🥈 Asian Games 2014
records: Record: 6 World Championship golds — no boxer male or female from India or Asia has matched this. Most decorated Indian boxer of all time.

name: Arundhati Choudhary
medals: 🥉 Asian Championships 2023; 🥈 CWG 2022 team; 🥇 National champion 2023–24
records: Asian bronze + CWG silver in same year (2022–23). Best: World No. 12 in 66kg.

name: Minakshi
medals: 🥇 Asian Championships 2022; 🥈 World Championships 2022
records: World Championships silver + Asian gold in same year at age 20. Filling Mary Kom's 48kg legacy. Personal record: World No. 3 in 48kg (2022).

name: Ankushita Boro
medals: 🥇 Asian Championships 2022; 🥈 CWG 2022; 🥉 Asian Games 2023
records: 3 international medals in 18 months across 3 different tournaments. Asian champion at 21.

name: Parveen Hooda
medals: 🥉 World Championships 2022; 🥉 CWG 2022
records: World Championships bronze at 21. WADA suspension ruled out Paris 2024. If reinstated, World No. 8 level.

name: Alfiya Pathan
medals: 🥉 World Championships 2021; 🥇 CWG 2022
records: World Championships bronze at 19 + CWG gold at 20. India's best-ever 81kg boxer — two senior international medals before age 21.

name: Sachin Siwach
medals: 🥉 Asian Championships 2022; 🥈 Youth World Championships 2022
records: Youth World silver + Asian bronze. Army boxer. Consistent transition to senior level.

name: Vishwanath Suresh
medals: 🥇 Youth World Championships 2024; 🥇 Asian Youth Championships 2024
records: Youth World + Asian double gold in 2024 — only Indian boxer to win both in same year at U20 level.

name: Rohit Tokas
medals: 🥇 Asian U22 Championships 2023; 🥈 Youth World Championships 2022
records: U22 Asian champion at 21. Direct pipeline successor to Nishant Dev.

name: Sanjeet
medals: 🥈 Asian Championships 2021; 🥉 Asian Games 2023
records: Career record: 2 Asian-level medals. India's most consistent 92kg boxer.

name: Govind Sahani
medals: 🥇 Youth World Championships 2023; 🥇 Asian Youth Championships 2023
records: World + Asian gold double at U17 level at age 16 — youngest Indian World champion in boxing history. 2032 Olympic target.

name: Mirabai Chanu
medals: 🥈 Tokyo 2020; 4th Paris 2024 (missed bronze by 1kg); 🥇 World Championships 2017; 🥇 CWG 2022; 🥈 World Championships 2025
records: At World Weightlifting Championships 2025 in Norway, won silver with 199kg total and set new national records in Clean & Jerk (115kg) and total lift (199kg). The Bridge Personal best total: 207kg (49kg category). National records: Snatch 88kg, C&J 119kg, Total 207kg (49kg). India's most decorated weightlifter.

name: Jeremy Lalrinnunga
medals: 🥇 Youth Olympics 2018 (first Indian Youth Olympic gold); 🥇 CWG 2022 (67kg — Games record 300kg); 🥇 Commonwealth Championships 2021
records: Still holds the world record in Youth World Championships 67kg with his performances. CRW Flags CWG record: 300kg total (140kg snatch + 160kg C&J) at Birmingham 2022. Youth Olympic champion. India's only Youth Olympic gold medallist.

name: Achinta Sheuli
medals: 🥇 CWG 2022 (Games record 313kg); 🥇 Commonwealth Championships 2021; 🥈 Asian Championships 2021
records: CWG record: 313kg total (143kg snatch + 170kg C&J) at Birmingham 2022 — Games record. Set new national records in Snatch (143kg) C&J (173kg) and total (316kg) at Commonwealth Championships 2021. Olympics

name: Bindyarani Devi
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022 (55kg); 🥇 Commonwealth Championships 2021; 🥉 Asian Championships 2022
records: Holds all three national records in women's 55kg — Snatch: 88kg (National Games 2025), C&J: 119kg, Total: 202kg (CWG 2022). ESPN Broke Mirabai's national snatch record at National Games 2025.

name: Sanket Mahadev Sargar
medals: 🥈 CWG 2022 (55kg — set Games record before injury forced him to lower attempts); 🥇 Commonwealth Championships 2021
records: CWG silver despite competing injured. Games record attempt: 113kg snatch. National record in 55kg.

name: Gyaneshwari Yadav
medals: 5th World Championships 2024; 🥇 National Championships 2024
records: Fifth-place finish at World Championships in Chanu's absence — total 186kg. Olympics Best performance without Mirabai present. National champion 2024. India's next 49kg hope.

name: Gurdeep Singh
medals: 🥉 CWG 2022; 🥇 Commonwealth Championships 2021; 🥉 Asian Championships 2021
records: National record: 390kg total (CWG 2022). India's best-ever super heavyweight lift. 3 international medals.

name: Harshada Garud
medals: 🥇 Youth World Championships 2023; 🥇 Asian Youth Championships 2023
records: Youth World record: lifted 153kg total (67kg snatch + 86kg C&J) at Youth Worlds 2023 — set Youth World Championship record. India's youngest active world champion.

name: Popy Hazarika
medals: 🥇 Junior World Championships 2023; 🥇 Asian Youth Championships 2022
records: Junior World Champion — lifted 203kg total at Junior Worlds 2023. Northeast India's most decorated young lifter.

name: Jhilli Dalabehera
medals: 🥈 Junior World Championships 2022; 🥇 Asian Youth Championships 2022
records: Personal best: 173kg total (78kg snatch + 95kg C&J). Junior World silver. Odisha-based — India's 2nd strongest active 49kg lifter.

name: Vanshaj
medals: 🥇 Youth World Championships 2024; 🥈 Asian Youth Championships 2024
records: Youth World record: 261kg total at Youth Worlds 2024 — set Youth World Championship record in 61kg. India's most exciting teenage male lifter.

name: Ajay Singh
medals: 🥈 Commonwealth Championships 2023; 🥉 Asian Championships 2023
records: Personal best: 330kg total in 73kg. National runner-up to Achinta. 2028 contender.

name: Preetismita Bhoi
medals: 🥇 Youth CWG Championships 2025 (44kg); 🥇 Commonwealth youth records in all 3 lifts
records: Set Commonwealth youth records in all three categories at Commonwealth Weightlifting Championships 2025. Olympics Three youth Commonwealth records in a single competition at age 17.

name: Dharmajyoti Dewgharia
medals: 🥇 Commonwealth Weightlifting Championships 2025 youth (56kg); set youth C&J + total Commonwealth records
records: Lifted 224kg (97kg snatch + 127kg C&J) — set youth Commonwealth records in C&J and total. Olympics Most decorated Indian youth male lifter in 2025.

name: Rakhi Halder
medals: 🥈 Commonwealth Championships 2022; 🥇 National champion 3x
records: National record in 71kg: 241kg total. India's best active 71kg lifter. 3x national champion.

name: Herojit Singh
medals: 🥉 Asian Championships 2022; 🥇 National champion 2023
records: Personal best: 285kg total in 61kg. National champion. Manipur Army lifter with strong 2028 pathway.

name: Deepak Lather
medals: 🥉 CWG 2018; Former national record holder 69kg
records: CWG bronze at 19 — still youngest Indian CWG weightlifting medallist at time of achievement. Personal best: 317kg total (69kg).

name: Tulasi Devi
medals: 🥉 Commonwealth Championships 2023; National champion 2022–23
records: Personal best: 195kg total in 55kg. National champion. CWG 2026 contender.

name: Sanjita Chanu
medals: 🥇 CWG 2014 + 2018; Former world No. 1
records: Career record: 2 CWG golds in same weight class — only Indian female lifter with back-to-back CWG titles. Former world No. 1 ranking.

name: Karnam Malleswari(legend/mentor)
medals: 🥉 Sydney 2000; 🥇 World Championships 1993 + 1994
records: Record: India's first female Olympic medallist. 2x World Champion — only Indian woman lifter to achieve this. Now SAI national coach.

name: Dhiraj Bommadevara
medals: Paris 2024 — 4th (mixed team); 🥇 World Cup 2024 Stage 1 men's team; 🥇 Asian Championships 2025 individual
records: Collected eight medals across individual, men's team and mixed team events in 2023 and 2024 World Cup stages. Drishti IAS First Indian male recurve Asian individual champion. ESPN World ranking: Top 10 recurve globally (2025). Personal best score: 699/720 (qualification round).

name: Deepika Kumari
medals: 4x Olympian; 🥈 World Cup 2024 Stage 1 Shanghai; 🥈 World Cup Final 2024; 🥇 CWG 2010 (2 golds)
records: Former world No. 1. Olympics Career record: 4 Olympic appearances — most by any Indian archer. Personal best: 686/720 (qualification round). World No. 1 held in 2011–12. Most decorated Indian woman archer.

name: Ankita Bhakat
medals: Paris 2024 4th (mixed team); 🥇 Asian Championships 2025 (individual)
records: First Indian women's recurve individual Asian champion. ESPN Record: India's first women's recurve Asian individual title. World ranking: Top 15 recurve women globally (2025). Personal best qualification score: 676/720.

name: Aditi Swami
medals: 🥇 World Championships 2023 (compound women); 🥇 Asian Games 2023; Multiple World Cup gold 2024
records: Non-Olympic compound archery events — reigning women's world champion Aditi Swami. Two CirclesRecord: Youngest-ever Indian to win a World Archery Championship (age 17 in 2023). World ranking: #2 compound women globally. Personal best: 150/150 (perfect score in compound).

name: Jyoti Surekha Vennam
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2023 (individual + team); 🥇 World Championships 2022 + 2023 (team); 🥈 World Cup Final 2024
records: Career record: 3 consecutive World Championship team golds (2019, 2022, 2023). Asian Games individual + team double in same Games. World ranking: #1 compound women (held 2022–23). Personal best: 150/150 compound.

name: Atanu Das
medals: 2x Olympian; 🥇 World Cup 2021 Stage 3; 🥇 Asian Championships 2019
records: World Cup gold medal at Stage 3, 2021 (Paris). Career record: 2 Olympic appearances + Asian champion. Personal best: 694/720. Preparing for LA 2028.

name: Tarundeep Rai
medals: 3x Olympian (Athens, Beijing, Paris 2024); 🥇 CWG 2006; 🥇 Asian Championships 2004
records: Record: 3 Olympic appearances — most by any Indian male archer. 20+ year international career (2003–2024). Arjuna Award 2006. India's only archer to compete at 3 Olympics in different decades.

name: Aditi / Compound women team
medals: 🥇 World Cup 2024 Stage 1 + 2 (women's compound team)
records: India's compound women's team won back-to-back World Cup stage golds in 2024 — first Indian women's compound team to achieve this.

name: Prathamesh Fuge
medals: 4th World Cup Final 2024 (just missed bronze); 🥇 Asian Championships 2023 (team)
records: Personal best: 149/150 compound (near-perfect score). Closest any Indian compound man has come to World Cup Final podium. Asian team champion.

name: Harvinder Singh
medals: 🥇 Paris 2024 Paralympics; 🥉 Tokyo 2020 Paralympics
records: Record: India's first Paralympic archery gold (Paris 2024). Only Indian para archer with two Paralympic medals. Personal best: 680/720 recurve. Pioneer of para archery in India.

name: Abhishek Verma
medals: 🥇 Asian Games 2018; 🥈 World Championships 2019 (team); Multiple World Cup medals
records: Record: India's first Asian Games compound gold(2018). Career: 10+ World Cup medals. Personal best: 149/150. Most experienced Indian compound archer.

name: Komalika Bari
medals: 🥇 World U18 Championships 2021; 🥇 Asia Cup 2022
records: Record: Youngest-ever Indian to win U18 World Archery Championship (age 18 in 2021). Personal best: 672/720. Strong LA 2028 candidate.

name: Pravin Jadhav
medals: Paris 2024 participant; 🥇 World Cup 2024 Stage 1 (men's team — beat Korea after 14 years)
records: Part of India's men's recurve team that clinched first World Cup gold in 14 years, defeating South Korea. Wikipedia Personal best: 688/720.

name: Ojas Deotale
medals: 🥇 World Cup 2023 Stage 3 (mixed team); 🥇 Asian Championships 2023 (team)
records: Personal best: 148/150 compound. World Cup gold at 21. Rising compound talent.

name: Priyansh

records: Personal best: 149/150 compound. World Cup Final silver at 18. Top-ranked junior compound archer in Asia (2024).

name: Bhajan Kaur
medals: Paris 2024 participant; 🥉 Asian Games 2023 (team); 🥇 World Cup 2024 Stage 1 (team)
records: Personal best: 668/720. Asian Games team bronze + World Cup gold in same year.

records: Part of India's back-to-back compound women's team World Cup wins. Personal best: 147/150.

name: Simranjeet Kaur

records: Personal best: 660/720. U20 Asian bronze. LA 2028 target.

name: Ridhi

records: Personal best: 645/720 (U17 — national record for the age group). India's top U17 recurve girl. 2032 Olympic target.

name: Manika Batra
medals: Paris 2024 R16; 🥇 CWG 2018 (women's singles); 🥉 Asian Games 2018 (mixed doubles); 🥇 Asian Championships 2024 (team)
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 24. First Indian woman to break into world's top 25 in singles. sportsboardindia Record: First Indian to reach Olympic singles R16 (Paris 2024). Most decorated Indian women's TT player.

name: Sreeja Akula
medals: Paris 2024 R16; 🥇 WTT Contender Lagos 2024; 🥇 CWG 2022 (mixed doubles with Sharath); 🥉 Asian Championships 2024 (team)
records: Highest-ever ranked Indian singles player — career-best World No. 21 (August 2024). Sports Authority of India First Indian woman to win a WTT Contender singles title. Invest IndiaRecord: World No. 21 — highest ranking ever by an Indian singles TT player (male or female).

name: Achanta Sharath Kamal
medals: 5x Olympian; 🥇 CWG 2022 (4 medals — singles, mixed doubles, men's doubles, team); 🥇 CWG 2018 (singles); 🥈 Asian Games 2022 (team)
records: Record 10x national champion. First Indian to win ITTF Pro Tour title. First Indian to win CWG singles gold. ESPN Career record: 4 medals at a single CWG (2022) — no Indian TT player has matched this. Best world ranking: #24 (2021).

name: Sathiyan Gnanasekaran
medals: 🥇 CWG 2018 (team); Paris 2024 team; 🥇 Asian Championships 2024 (mixed doubles); ITTF Belgium Open 2017
records: Career-best world ranking: World No. 25 (2019). Highest-ever ranking for an Indian men's singles player for 5 years. First Indian to win WTT mixed doubles title at senior level.

name: Harmeet Desai
medals: Paris 2024 men's team; 🥇 CWG 2018 (team); 🥇 CWG 2022 (team); Multiple ITTF Challenge titles
records: Career record: 2 CWG team golds (2018 + 2022). Career-best ranking: World No. 60. Consistent top-75 performer for 6 years.

name: Archana Kamath
medals: Paris 2024 women's team QF; 🥇 WTT Contender Lagos 2024 (doubles with Sreeja); 🥉 Asian Championships 2024 (team)
records: Career-best ranking: World No. 52. Won WTT Contender doubles title at 22. Part of Paris 2024 QF women's team.

name: Sutirtha Mukherjee
medals: 🥇 CWG 2022 (women's team); 🥈 Asian Championships 2024 (doubles with Ayhika)
records: With Ayhika Mukherjee, ranked world No. 17 in women's doubles. Taylor & Francis OnlineCareer-best singles ranking: World No. 84. Best doubles ranking: World No. 17 (with Ayhika) — highest doubles ranking by Indian women's pair.

name: Ayhika Mukherjee
medals: 🥇 CWG 2022 (women's team); 🥈 Asian Championships 2024 (doubles with Sutirtha)
records: Doubles world ranking No. 17 with Sutirtha. Taylor & Francis Online World No. 17 doubles — matches Sutirtha's record as India's highest-ranked women's doubles pair ever.

name: Manav Thakkar

records: Career-best world ranking: World No. 53. Junior World Cup gold at 16. Record: India's second-highest ranked men's singles player after Sharath (at peak).

name: Yashansh Mulye

records: Record: World U15 Champion at age 14 — youngest Indian table tennis world champion at any age group. Currently ranked inside world top 100 juniors. 2032 Olympic target.

records: Career-best ranking: World No. 89. Multiple WTT Challenge titles. Consistent domestic and international performer.

records: Career-best ranking: World No. 95. Asian junior champion at 18. Strong women's singles pipeline.

records: Career-best ranking: World No. 130 at age 19 — fastest climb among Indian U21 men's singles players in 5 years.

records: Commonwealth Championships gold. Career-best ranking: World No. 110. Rising U21 star.

records: CWG team gold 2022. Career-best ranking: World No. 85. Consistent national team contributor.

records: Asian U19 champion at 18. Career-best junior ranking: World No. 22 (juniors). Direct successor to Manav Thakkar pipeline.

records: Asian junior silver. Career-best: World No. 120. Northeast India's most decorated TT player after Sharath era.

name: Krittwika Sinha Roy

records: Career-best ranking: World No. 125. WTT Youth title at 17. One of India's fastest-rising junior women.

records: Career-best ranking: World No. 95. Commonwealth champion. Consistent senior domestic performer.

records: Career-best junior ranking: World No. 5 (U15). India's youngest active international. 2032 Olympic pipeline.
